fields.summary: Extend the resource class to support ports management

issuetype.description: A new feature of the product, which has yet to be developed.
issuetype.name: New Feature

fields.description: Just like the vcores and memory, ports is an important resource for job to allocate. We should add the ports management logic to yarn. It can support the user to allocate two jobs(with same port requirement) to different machines.

status.description: A patch for this issue has been uploaded to JIRA by a contributor.
status.name: Patch Available

issuetype.description: An improvement or enhancement to an existing feature or task.
issuetype.name: Improvement

comments.body: For this specific use case, it may more make sense to align with anti-affinity scheduling.
Hi, [~grey],  can you give more detail info about anti-affinity scheduling? Currently we are using the 2.7.0 version, which version is it published?
not same as anti-affinity scheduling, just bring one way to manager ports as resource in yarn.
